title: Stewartia laotica
taxonomy: Stewartia laotica belongs to the family Theaceae and is a species of flowering plant native to Laos. It is part of the genus Stewartia, which includes multiple species of deciduous trees found in Asia. The plant is characterized by its showy white flowers and attractive bark. It is closely related to other species within the genus, such as Stewartia monadelpha and Stewartia pseudocamellia. The classification of Stewartia laotica places it among the flowering plants in the Campanulidae clade.

intro: Stewartia laotica is a plant species native to Laos, belonging to the family Theaceae, which is a genus of flowering plants commonly known as the "orchid trees" or "deciduous stewartias." The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its use in traditional medicine to treat fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Stewartia laotica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The most common herbal preparations made with Stewartia laotica include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections.
